text:
TREASURY ACTION BRIEF — Ridgewater / Meridian USD 18,400,000 Wire
==================================================================
*(Revision notes plus revised draft for the next pass. Source IDs verified against the Source Index headers.)*

---

## REVISION NOTES (what this pass changes)

- GO reframed as conditional and **not** the realistic base case; STOP/HOLD is the base case, supported by an explicit time budget.
- "One-of-two Treasury approval" now attributed strictly to the **derived** dashboard and flagged as uncorroborated by any authoritative approval log.
- Holding notice converted from "send" to "draft + route for approval"; no presumed Compliance/Legal approval.
- Added explicit recovery/exposure asymmetry (USD 18,400,000 irreversible vs. ≥1-business-day reversible delay).
- Added omitted risks: mis-worded notice, internal mistaken-release click-through, self-serving production claims, attestation-reliance.
- All ten source IDs verified verbatim; Section 10 completed with full required disclaimer.

---

## 1. Bottom-line recommendation

Do **not** release the wire and do **not** send a final payment confirmation before the 16:30 ET cutoff. On current evidence, the realistic outcome is HOLD, not release. Treasury should prepare the wire package and place it in **hold queue without release** (a reversible step) per S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE, and route the unresolved gates to Compliance/Legal/Treasury. A **limited holding notice** may go to the supplier **only after** Compliance/Legal approve its non-committal wording (S1_TREASURY_PAYMENT_RELEASE_POLICY); it is not yet approved and must not be sent as drafted. Release becomes permissible **only** if current-day rescreen, UBO disposition, Compliance release, and the second Treasury approval all clear before cutoff — a sequence nothing in the packet indicates is close.

## 2. What is happening and why it matters now

Ridgewater is deciding whether to release USD 18,400,000 to Meridian Supply Pte Ltd. before a same-day cutoff. Prior-day vendor-entity screening was clear (S2_PRIOR_DAY_SANCTIONS_SCREEN_CLEARANCE), and vendor master, AP history, and bank callback are complete (S4_VENDOR_MASTER_BANK_CALLBACK_LOG; S7_AP_VENDOR_HISTORY_ACTIVE_STATUS). But the current-day rescreen, beneficial owner (UBO) disposition, and Compliance release are open (S5_COMPLIANCE_RELEASE_QUEUE_STATUS), and a current-day review was triggered by an intermediary-bank change and an 82% UBO fuzzy match (S6_CURRENT_DAY_UBO_INTERMEDIARY_ESCALATION). A business sponsor and the supplier are pressing for release/confirmation today (S8_BUSINESS_SPONSOR_SUPPLIER_URGENCY_NOTE). The decision matters now because crossing from hold queue to released is irreversible absent separate bank cancellation confirmation (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE).

## 3. Strongest evidence

Policy is unambiguous: cross-border wires above USD 5,000,000 require same-business-day screening, documented UBO review, callback, dual Treasury approval, **and Compliance release** before payment release; vendor master and payment history do **not** substitute (S1_TREASURY_PAYMENT_RELEASE_POLICY). The authoritative current-status source confirms release item RQ-8841 is open, rescreen pending, UBO pending, no Compliance release, payment release **not authorized** (S5_COMPLIANCE_RELEASE_QUEUE_STATUS). The escalation record requires reviewer disposition before release and requires the **new intermediary bank** to be in the screened-party set before any final confirmation (S6_CURRENT_DAY_UBO_INTERMEDIARY_ESCALATION). The bank note confirms reversible preparation is available and the hold→released move is the irreversible action (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE). These four strong sources converge: the controlling gates are unmet.

## 4. Weak, stale, missing, or conflicting evidence

S2_PRIOR_DAY_SANCTIONS_SCREEN_CLEARANCE is stale and tempting: prior-day, vendor-entity-only, no current refresh, no UBO, no new intermediary, no post-cutoff jurisdiction note — useful history, not a release. S3_DERIVED_PAYMENT_RISK_DASHBOARD looks precise but is **derived**, with calculated or blank fields and a footnote that green "does not mean sanctions release." The "Treasury dual approval one of two" state appears **only** in this derived dashboard and is **not corroborated** by any authoritative approval log; treat it as indicative, not confirmed. S9_WEAK_SUPPLIER_SANCTIONS_ATTESTATION is a self-serving supplier assurance naming no beneficial owners, no intermediary screening, no reviewer — it cannot substitute for Ridgewater screening. S8_BUSINESS_SPONSOR_SUPPLIER_URGENCY_NOTE is classified contradictory/complicating; its production-impact claims are asserted by interested parties, with no independent operational evidence in the packet. Missing entirely: current-day rescreen result, UBO disposition, Compliance release, the second Treasury approval, any bank cancellation guarantee, and any contract waiver.

## 5. Calculations / payment-risk interpretation

**Time budget.** The latest authoritative timestamps are 15:12 ET (S5_COMPLIANCE_RELEASE_QUEUE_STATUS) and 15:08 ET (S6_CURRENT_DAY_UBO_INTERMEDIARY_ESCALATION), leaving roughly 78 minutes to the 16:30 ET cutoff (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE). Within that window, **four** distinct items must complete: current-day rescreen, UBO disposition, Compliance release, and a second Treasury approval. Nothing in the packet shows any of these near completion. STOP/HOLD is therefore the **base case**, not the exception.

**The 82% match is undispositioned.** The packet does not tell us whether 82% is a true hit, false positive, or noise (S6_CURRENT_DAY_UBO_INTERMEDIARY_ESCALATION; S5_COMPLIANCE_RELEASE_QUEUE_STATUS). Releasing now means acting on an **unresolved** match, not a cleared one.

**Recovery asymmetry.** A wrong release puts the full USD 18,400,000 at irreversible risk unless the bank separately confirms cancellation — which the packet does not establish (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE). A hold costs at most a ≥1-business-day settlement delay on the **same** path. The exposures are not symmetric: one side is irreversible loss of principal plus sanctions liability; the other is a recoverable delay.

## 6. Practical response options

1. **HOLD + prepare (recommended).** Build, validate, and upload the wire to hold queue without release (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE); route open gates to Compliance/Treasury (S5_COMPLIANCE_RELEASE_QUEUE_STATUS; S6_CURRENT_DAY_UBO_INTERMEDIARY_ESCALATION).
2. **Draft limited holding notice → route for approval.** Do not send until Compliance/Legal approve non-committal wording (S1_TREASURY_PAYMENT_RELEASE_POLICY).
3. **Conditional GO (unlikely today).** Release **only** after all four gates clear pre-cutoff. Not supported by current evidence.
4. **Release now / send confirmation now.** Rejected — violates S1 and S5; acts on an undispositioned match.
5. **Rely on supplier attestation.** Rejected — inverts the screening requirement (S1; S9_WEAK_SUPPLIER_SANCTIONS_ATTESTATION).

## 7. Risks of acting

Releasing or confirming now would breach policy gates (S1_TREASURY_PAYMENT_RELEASE_POLICY) and the Compliance non-authorization (S5_COMPLIANCE_RELEASE_QUEUE_STATUS), and would transmit funds to a payment chain whose new intermediary bank is not yet in the screened set and whose UBO match is undispositioned (S6_CURRENT_DAY_UBO_INTERMEDIARY_ESCALATION) — potential sanctions exposure on USD 18,400,000 that is irreversible absent bank cancellation (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE). Secondary acting risks: (a) a holding notice that drifts into commitment language functions as a de facto confirmation, which S1 permits only post-release or post-approval; (b) an uploaded-but-held wire invites an internal click-through to "released" under sponsor pressure (S10) — release rights must not be exercised until GO triggers are verified; (c) relying on S9_WEAK_SUPPLIER_SANCTIONS_ATTESTATION to beat the cutoff would substitute a self-serving assurance for required screening.

## 8. Risks of waiting

The sponsor warns of component holds and line stoppage, and the supplier says it needs confirmation today to allocate inventory (S8_BUSINESS_SPONSOR_SUPPLIER_URGENCY_NOTE). These are real commercial risks but are **claimed by interested parties**; no independent production-impact evidence is in the packet. Missing the cutoff likely delays settlement ≥1 business day (S10_BANK_CUTOFF_AND_HOLD_QUEUE_NOTICE), with possible supplier-relationship and default friction. Four-year clean AP history (S7_AP_VENDOR_HISTORY_ACTIVE_STATUS) suggests the relationship can absorb a short, well
